scripts.dev:cap: cross-env CAPACITOR=true node scripts/web.mjs vite dev --host
scripts.prebuild: node scripts/web.mjs svelte-kit sync && npm run gen:icons && npm run gen:releases
scripts.prebuild:cap: node scripts/web.mjs svelte-kit sync && npm run gen:icons && npm run gen:releases
scripts.build:cap: cross-env CAPACITOR=true node scripts/web.mjs vite build && node --experimental-strip-types --disable-warning=ExperimentalWarning scripts/strip-native-assets.mjs
scripts.test: npm run test:unit && npm run test:e2e
scripts.perf:serve: cross-env PUBLIC_ENABLE_DEV_HARNESS=true node scripts/web.mjs vite preview --host --port 4173
scripts.test:e2e:headed: cross-env SLOWMO=500 node scripts/web.mjs playwright test --headed
scripts.cap:android: npm run cap:sync && cap open android
scripts.cap:ios: npm run cap:sync && cap open ios
scripts.android:emulator: npm run cap:sync && cap run android --target Pixel_7_Pro_API_33
scripts.android:run:device: npm run cap:sync && cross-env ANDROID_SERIAL=R5CY128YMGF node scripts/gradle.mjs :app:installDebug
scripts.ios:build: npm run cap:sync && cd ios/App && xcodebuild -scheme App -configuration Debug -destination "generic/platform=iOS Simulator" -derivedDataPath build build
scripts.ios:run: npm run cap:sync && cap run ios
scripts.ios:run:emulator: npm run cap:sync && cap run ios --target C6012C49-AA93-4869-B3A6-E47C9EAAC567
scripts.ios:run:device: npm run cap:sync && cap run ios --target 00008103-0006202E3CF1001E
scripts.ios:archive: npm run cap:sync && cd ios/App && xcodebuild -scheme App -configuration Release -destination generic/platform=iOS -archivePath build/App.xcarchive -xcconfig ../local.xcconfig -allowProvisioningUpdates archive
scripts.ios:ipa: npm run ios:archive && cd ios/App && xcodebuild -exportArchive -archivePath build/App.xcarchive -exportOptionsPlist ExportOptions.plist -exportPath build/ipa -allowProvisioningUpdates

scripts-info.info: Lists every npm script with its description (this output)
scripts-info.postinstall: Apply the @capacitor/cli Windows gradlew patch via patch-package (ADR-0011)
scripts-info.predev:netlify: Checks the Netlify CLI is on PATH, logged in, and linked before dev:netlify (Blobs needs auth), with a hint if not
scripts-info.dev:netlify: Dev server with the /api/* serverless functions (Netlify Dev)
scripts-info.dev:host: Dev server exposed on the local network (for phones and other devices)
scripts-info.dev:cap: Dev server in native mode (CAPACITOR=true) on the network, for android:live
scripts-info.dev:tunnel: Cloud session only: start dev + a chisel reverse tunnel and print the public phone-preview URL (needs TUNNEL_AUTH; see docs/CLOUD.md, ADR-0021)
scripts-info.dev:kill: Kill any stale dev servers holding the dev ports (vite 5173, netlify dev 8888)
scripts-info.prebuild: svelte-kit sync + gen:icons + gen:releases before build
scripts-info.prebuild:cap: svelte-kit sync + gen:icons + gen:releases before build:cap
scripts-info.build:cap: Native static build (CAPACITOR=true, adapter-static); strips web-only coloring-book assets
scripts-info.preview: Serve the production web build locally
scripts-info.precheck: Generate .svelte-kit types (svelte-kit sync) so type checking can run standalone
scripts-info.check:assets: Validate that every coloring-book asset exists on disk (auto-runs before release)
scripts-info.lint: ESLint over the whole repo minus the ignores in eslint.config.js (flat config; enforces runes-only, no legacy stores)
scripts-info.format: Format the whole repo with Prettier minus .prettierignore (md/json/yml excluded there for now)
scripts-info.format:check: Check Prettier formatting without writing — what CI runs
scripts-info.test: Unit (Vitest) + E2E (Playwright) — what CI runs on every push
scripts-info.perf:web: Autonomous performance profile of the web app: builds the production bundle with PERF_MARKS=true, serves it via vite preview, drives a scripted toddler session (multi-finger draw, color/size changes, erase, undo, clear) in headless Chromium under 4x CPU throttle while recording a Chrome trace + runtime metrics, then writes trace.json/metrics.json/summary.json/report.md + a screenshot to perf-profiles/. Flags: --device=phone|tablet|desktop, --throttle=N, --no-build (reuse last build)
scripts-info.perf:web:raw: perf:web without CPU throttling (raw desktop speed)
scripts-info.perf:android: Autonomous performance profile of the REAL Capacitor WebView on a connected Android emulator/device (local-only): rebuilds + installs the native app with PERF_MARKS=true, finds the WebView's DevTools socket, forwards it, connects Playwright over CDP, and runs the same toddler session + capture as perf:web (no CPU throttle — the device is the target). --no-build profiles the installed app as-is. Writes to perf-profiles/
scripts-info.perf:ios: Autonomous performance profile in Playwright's WebKit (the same WebKit+JavaScriptCore engine the iOS WKWebView runs), driving the production preview build. WebKit has no CDP trace, so this captures the engine.* user-timing marks + FPS (not the main-thread/CPU breakdown); it profiles the engine, not the Simulator app. For device-accurate numbers, record a Safari Web Inspector Timeline against the running Simulator and feed it to perf:analyze. Writes to perf-profiles/
scripts-info.perf:undo: Focused undo/keyframe profile (ADR-0033/0035): builds the production bundle with PERF_MARKS=true, serves it via vite preview with the /dev/engine harness unlocked, and drives three deliberately-shaped sessions (12 long squiggles; 12 short dot/dash marks; a mix) in headless Chromium under 4x CPU throttle. Records per scenario — via getUndoDebug() — the retained command count, keyframe count, and peak op count, plus engine.draw/keyframe/undo timing and JS-heap footprint while history is resident. Writes trace.json/summary.json/report.md + undo-scenarios.json/.md to perf-profiles/. Flags: --throttle=N, --no-throttle, --no-build. Re-runnable unchanged against the snapshot engine for a side-by-side comparison
scripts-info.perf:analyze: Re-run the trace analyzer over a saved profile dir or trace.json (regenerates summary.json + report.md without re-capturing — the path a native-exported trace takes): npm run perf:analyze -- perf-profiles/<dir>
scripts-info.perf:serve: Serve the current production build on the LAN (0.0.0.0:4173) with the /dev/* harness routes unlocked (PUBLIC_ENABLE_DEV_HARNESS=true), so a real iPad/phone on the same Wi-Fi can reach http://<mac-lan-ip>:4173/ to record real input (scripts/perf/ipad-recorder.js) or /dev/engine. Build first with: cross-env PERF_MARKS=true PUBLIC_ENABLE_DEV_HARNESS=true npm run build. Stop it before perf:replay (same port). See the profiling skill's ipad-device-profiling.md
scripts-info.perf:replay: Replay a REAL finger-input recording (captured on-device with scripts/perf/ipad-recorder.js) through the engine under the profiler, so the harness measures actual human strokes instead of synthetic squiggles: opens /dev/engine, sizes the canvas to the recorded device, replays the pointer stream + UI actions at recorded timing (real frame pacing), captures a CDP trace + engine marks, and reports how your input was stored (keyframes/commands/op counts). npm run perf:replay -- --recording=<file.json> [--turbo] [--throttle=N] [--no-build]. Writes trace/report + replay-summary to perf-profiles/
scripts-info.perf:sweep: Empirically sweep the ADR-0036 stroke-simplification tolerance vs. fidelity: draws a grid of non-overlapping synthetic strokes (live), forces one rebuild-from-stored-ops (the all-strokes replay a single undo triggers), and diffs per cell — AA-tolerant moved-ink + ink-extent delta — across a range of tolerances (scale 0 = no thinning = representation floor), reporting segments replayed, rebuild ms, the fidelity/perf curve, and a recommended setting. Uses the dev-gated setSimplifyParams seam so one build covers every setting. npm run perf:sweep [-- --no-build]. Writes sweep.json/.md + live/rebuilt screenshots to perf-profiles/
scripts-info.perf:ios:analyze: Analyze a Safari Web Inspector Timeline export from a REAL iOS device (a different format from perf:analyze — {recording:{records,markers}}, mark-only, ring-buffered, ~1ms clock). Recovers per-op main-thread cost (undo/keyframe/commit) from the enclosing timeline record, plus paint/composite and frame stats: npm run perf:ios:analyze -- <export>.json. See the profiling skill's ipad-device-profiling.md
scripts-info.test:e2e:headed: Playwright with browser visible, slowed down (SLOWMO=500)
scripts-info.test:android: Android smoke test, self-contained: boots a headless emulator, builds + installs, runs Maestro, tears down
scripts-info.test:android:device: Maestro smoke test against an already-running emulator/device (what CI uses; same flow works on a booted iOS simulator)
scripts-info.test:ios: iOS smoke test, self-contained: boots a simulator, builds + installs, runs Maestro, tears down (macOS-only)
scripts-info.test:api:smoke: API contract smoke test, self-contained: boots a throwaway vite dev with test env, exercises the admin auth flow + a public oracle against the documented /api/* shapes, tears down (no Gemini/Blobs needed)
scripts-info.test:blobs:smoke: Deploy smoke test (ADR-0025): against a real Netlify deploy (BLOBS_SMOKE_URL + ADMIN_ACCESS_TOKEN), asserts persistent:true and round-trips a token through Blobs to prove the deployed function has the Blobs context; cleans up after itself
scripts-info.redteam: MANUAL AI-safety red-team (ADR-0023): boots a throwaway vite dev, decrypts the corpus, sends each safe-*/block-* drawing to a real Gemini call, and writes an HTML input->output report to tests/redteam/output/ for human review (opens in your browser). Pass ids to run a subset (npm run redteam -- block-gun) to iterate on one drawing. Real GEMINI_API_KEY + REDTEAM_FIXTURE_KEY required; excluded from npm test by design
scripts-info.redteam:encrypt: Encrypt tests/redteam/source/*.png -> tests/redteam/encrypted/*.enc (commit the .enc); needs REDTEAM_FIXTURE_KEY
scripts-info.gen:icons: Generate src/lib/components/icon-names.d.ts (typed IconName union) from the SVGs in src/lib/icons/ (auto-runs before build)
scripts-info.gen:releases: Generate src/lib/releases.json + fastlane store changelogs from releases/*.md (auto-runs before build)
scripts-info.gen:large-image: Regenerate static/large-image.png by replaying large-image.svg onto the live canvas (Playwright)
scripts-info.gen:style-covers: Regenerate the AI style thumbnails in static/styles/ from source.svg via Gemini (needs GEMINI_API_KEY; -- --style X --temperature N for one style / different takes)
scripts-info.gen:coloring-fills: Generate flat-color twins of the coloring pages in static/coloring/ via Gemini, preserving the exact outlines (needs GEMINI_API_KEY; -- page-or-category --samples N --temperature N)
scripts-info.gen:coloring-sheet: Build .coloring-samples/review-sheet.html — a tap-to-flip B&W⇄colored review of the generated twins (-- category… to filter)
scripts-info.gen:coloring-thumbs: Regenerate the -thumb.webp grid thumbnails beside every coloring cover/page for the picker (-- category… to filter)
scripts-info.gen:shots: Regenerate the store screenshots + feature graphic for both stores into store-assets/ (Playwright)
scripts-info.cap:sync: build:cap + copy web assets & plugins into the native projects
scripts-info.cap:android: cap:sync + open the Android project in Android Studio
scripts-info.cap:ios: cap:sync + open the iOS project in Xcode (macOS-only)
scripts-info.android:setup: One-time emulator setup: installs the API 33 system image and creates the Pixel_7_Pro_API_33 AVD
scripts-info.android:emulator: cap:sync + build, install, and launch on the Pixel_7_Pro_API_33 emulator
scripts-info.android:live: Run on the emulator with live reload against the dev server on port 5173
scripts-info.android:run: Build + install the debug app onto the connected device/emulator
scripts-info.android:run:device: cap:sync + build, install onto the physical 'SM-S938U1' phone (ANDROID_SERIAL=R5CY128YMGF; use when an emulator is also connected — find your serial with adb:devices)
scripts-info.android:verify: Verify the release AAB's signature (expect 'jar verified')
scripts-info.android:open: Reveal the release bundle folder in the OS file manager
scripts-info.ios:build: Debug build for the iOS Simulator → ios/App/build/Build/Products/Debug-iphonesimulator/App.app (macOS-only)
scripts-info.ios:run: cap:sync + build, install, and launch, prompting to choose the iOS simulator or connected device (macOS-only)
scripts-info.ios:run:emulator: cap:sync + build, install, and launch on the iPad mini (A17 Pro) simulator (macOS-only; use ios:run to pick a device)
scripts-info.ios:run:device: cap:sync + build, install, and launch on the physical 'Kyle’s iPad' device (macOS-only; needs signing + a trusted developer cert — see the mobile guide)
scripts-info.ios:live: Run on a simulator/device with live reload against the dev server on port 5173 (macOS-only; pair with dev:cap)
scripts-info.ios:archive: Release archive (signed; needs the Apple Developer team set in Xcode) → ios/App/build/App.xcarchive (macOS-only)
scripts-info.ios:ipa: ios:archive + export the App Store .ipa → ios/App/build/ipa/ (macOS-only; see the mobile guide)
scripts-info.ios:open: Reveal the exported .ipa folder in the OS file manager
scripts-info.ios:clean: Xcode clean of the App scheme (macOS-only)
scripts-info.adb:reverse: Forward the phone's port 5173 to the desktop dev server (re-run after each USB reconnect)
scripts-info.update:browserslist: Refresh the browserslist database (caniuse-lite) to clear outdated-browserslist warnings
scripts-info.release: Cut a release from releases/<version>.md: bump versions, commit, tag, push (use the /release command to draft notes first)
scripts-info.perf:units: Per-stroke fidelity unit tests for the drawing engine: draws each of a battery of strokes (synthetic primitives dot→scribble + every stroke extracted from the saved real sessions) live, forces one rebuild-from-stored-ops, and STRICTLY diffs (0px exact ink mismatch, worst coherent shift distance, extent delta), writing per-stroke live/rebuilt/diff PNGs and a pass/fail table. Verifies undo is pixel-identical. npm run perf:units [-- --no-build --filter=<name> --enabled --mode=midpoint|spline --no-reduce]
